name: Garden door
description: A discrete secondary doorway that connects an interior service room (kitchen, mudroom, pantry, scullery) to the garden, herb bed, or back yard. Read as an everyday working threshold rather than a ceremonial entry: a single, unornamented door punched into the rear wall, optionally flanked by tall narrow vertical windows that admit morning light without sacrificing privacy. A low optional threshold step (a single slab or stair tread) marks the transition from finished floor to soil, and the immediate outside cell is reserved for a planter or herb bed so the door opens onto a soft, cultivated zone rather than bare lawn. Style variants stay restrained — plain oak, weathered spruce, or whitewashed planks — never grand hardware.
